zh.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux是Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester和Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil的女兒，於1120年出生在法國豪-諾門迪艾蒙特福吉素-利斯爾。她和Simon de Montfort III至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1169年在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux是Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester和Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil的女儿，于1120年出生在法国豪-諾門迪艾蒙特福吉素-利斯爾。她和Simon de Montfort III至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1169年在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Rogero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Cayetano, Jose, Silva.
Spanish: from the personal name Rogero (see Roger ).
Catalan (Rogeró): from a pet form of the personal name Roger .

variants.name: Rieck
meanings: South German: from a pet form of the personal name Ru(o)diger, a compound of Old High German hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + gēr ‘spear, lance’ (see Roger ).
North German: from a short form of a personal name containing the element rīc ‘power(ful)’.
Germanized form of Sorbian Rjek: nickname from Upper Sorbian rjek ‘hero’. Compare Reck .

variants.name: Rogelio
meanings: Spanish; Portuguese (Rogélio): from the personal name Rogelio, Portuguese Rogélio, of ancient Germanic origin (see Roger ).

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Roger  .

variants.name: Rogers
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Roger , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Ruaidhrí (see Rorie ).

History: Thomas Rogers (c. 1587–1621), born in London, England, was among the Pilgrim Fathers who sailed on the Mayflower in 1620. He died during the first winter at Plymouth Colony, but his son Joseph survived and married, and was later joined in MA by his brother John. This name was subsequently brought to North America independently by many different bearers.

variants.name: Rogel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Jose, Jose Luis, Juan, Marcos, Margarita, Wilfredo, Adilia, Alejandro, Ana Gladys, Ana Maria, Anacleto.
Spanish: from the personal name Rogel, an equivalent of French Roger .

Americanized form of Slovenian Rogelj: nickname or topographic name from rogelj ‘horn, point’, figuratively ‘tall young man’, as a topographic name probably denoting someone who lived e.g. on a horn-shaped spur of a hill. The surname in the form Rogel is found in Slovenia, too, where it is a less common variant.

variants.name: Prosser
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Rhosier ‘son of Roger’ (see Roger and Rosser ).

variants.name: Rogerson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Roger + -son.

variants.name: Ritger
meanings: Altered form of German and Dutch Rutger or Rütger, from the personal name Rudiger (see Roger ).

variants.name: Rogge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Bernhard, Elke, Hans, Helmut, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Roger  .
North German and Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a grower of rye or a baker of rye bread, from Low German, Dutch rogge ‘rye’.

nameMeanings.name: Mathilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Mechtild
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mechtilde
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

en.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux was born in 1120, in Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Upper Normandy, France as the daughter of Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester and Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Simon de Montfort III. She died in 1169, in Leicester, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux est née en 1120, à Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France, fille de Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester et Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Simon de Montfort III. Elle est décédée en 1169, à Leicester, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux wurde 1120 in Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich, als Tochter von Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester und Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil geboren. Sie hatte mit Simon de Montfort III mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1169 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Leicester, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux è nata il nel 1120 nel Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia come figlia di Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester e Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Simon de Montfort III. Morì nel 1169, nel Leicester, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreauxは1120年にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアモントフォート-スル-リスルでRobert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of LeicesterとAmice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuilの娘として生まれた。彼女はSimon de Montfort IIIとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1169年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアレアイセスターで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux(은)는 1120년 프랑스 하테-노먼디 우르 몽포트-수-리슬일에서 Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester(와)과 Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Simon de Montfort III(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1169년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 레이스스터에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux nasceu em 1120, em Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, França filha de Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester e Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Simon de Montfort III. Ela faleceu em 1169, em Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux родилась в 1120 г. в местности монтфорт-сур-рисл, эур, хауте-норманди, Франция. Её родителей звали Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester и Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Simon de Montfort III. Она умерла в 1169 г. в местности лисестир, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Mathilde De Beaumont-Le Roger d'Evreaux nació en 1120, en Montfort-sur-Risle, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia como hija de Robert de Beaumont 2nd Lord of Leicester y Amice De Gael Heiress Of Breteuil. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Simon de Montfort III. Murió en 1169, en Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 49 años.
